meta.supersedes_resolution_in: WO-APEX-SP-A.0-STUCK-001 (Option A attempted; further iteration deemed structurally non-converging at pass M)

title: Formally split SP-A.0 into SP-A.0a (inventory only) + SP-A.0b (operational policy contributions, feeder to SP-A.2)
rationale: Option A's tightened spec underwent 12 adversarial-review passes without reaching silent-twice. Each pass produced 2-4 fresh substantive findings; severities oscillated around medium with occasional highs; the spec ratcheted in real improvements at every step but never reached zero residual. Diagnosis: codex adversarial-review is structurally non-converging on a paper spec carrying any operational density (locking, drift, hashing rules, rescue counters, restart cycles, no-deferral DoDs). Silent-twice is reachable only on artifacts the reviewer cannot find a fresh angle on â€” a SHA-pinned inventory of nine files is that artifact; a 100-line operational spec is not.

SP-A.0a.title: Authority-chain inventory (SHA-pinned)
SP-A.0a.scope: Load the 9 authority-chain files listed in 00_KICKOFF.md Step 1; capture full SHA-256 over raw bytes; verify by re-hash on read; ship the inventory file as the sole substantive artifact.
SP-A.0a.inventory_path: apex/docs/spec/SPEC-SP-A.0.preflight-inventory.md (already authored under SP-A.0; carried over verbatim with full 64-hex SHAs)
SP-A.0a.expected_convergence: silent-twice in â‰¤2 passes; the inventory itself is binary-verifiable
SP-A.0a.pr_strategy: local cert tag now; PR deferred via WO-APEX-REPO-RECONCILE-001

SP-A.0b.title: Operational policy contributions (feeder to SP-A.2)
SP-A.0b.scope: Single doc at apex/docs/policy/SP-A.0b-policy-contributions.md aggregating the operational rules generated during SP-A.0 passes A-M (locking, drift, hashing, rescue-counter, restart-cycle, no-deferral DoD norms, etc.). NOT reviewed as a per-phase spec; not subject to its own silent-twice loop.
SP-A.0b.feeder_target: SP-A.2 (Doctrine validation + lock v1.0). The silent-twice loop runs against the locked v1.0 doctrine after these contributions are merged in, not against this policy doc itself.
SP-A.0b.cert_close_dependency: SP-A.0b cert closes when SP-A.2 cert closes â€” SP-A.0b is a feeder, not a terminal phase. Its INDEX status reflects this (IN-PROGRESS-FEEDER until SP-A.2 closes, then DONE).
SP-A.0b.source_material: The 14 findings logged during SP-A.0 passes A-M (CDX-SP-A.0-* + AV-* + F-* + SP-A.0-* + SP-A0-* under various IDs across the 13 transcripts in apex_governance/codex_runs/SP-A.0/). Each finding becomes a policy bullet in the contributions doc with its rule-shape extracted.

index_changes: SP-A.0 row â†’ CLOSED-SPLIT, with cert column = 'see WO-APEX-SP-A.0-SPLIT-001'
Add SP-A.0a row (TODO at split, IN-PROGRESS while running, DONE on cert)
Add SP-A.0b row (IN-PROGRESS-FEEDER; closes with SP-A.2)
follow_on_obligations: SP-A.2 must absorb SP-A.0b's contributions when locking the doctrine v1.0; the silent-twice loop at SP-A.2 runs against the merged ruleset.

PHASE_TEMPLATE.md and PLAN_CRITERIA.md may receive amendments via the SP-A.2 doctrine lock to formalise the 'paper-primary phase exemption' for the QA-doc requirement and the 'spec-complexity ceiling for silent-twice' rule discovered empirically here.
supersedes_actions: WO-APEX-SP-A.0-STUCK-001 closes with this WO as the resolution. The earlier 'Option A' attempt is preserved in the codex_runs transcripts as evidence; it did not converge but produced the policy material that SP-A.0b now carries.

triage_note.observation: follow_on_obligation "SP-A.2 must absorb SP-A.0b's contributions when locking the doctrine v1.0" was substantively met by 2026-05-25 lock of docs/doctrine/APEX_DOCTRINE_v1.0.md (v1.0-PROVISIONAL). SP-A.0b-policy-contributions.md was annotated SUPERSEDED-BY-LOCK by Codex during WO-APEX-PROMOTE-DOCTRINE-003. Formal status flip to CLOSED is deferred until SP-A.2 silent-twice loop ratifies the lock to v1.0-LOCKED; until then status remains OPEN as a tracking record.
